## Review: ddx-d118f1bf iter 1

### Verdict: APPROVE

### AC Grades

| # | Item | Grade | Evidence |
|---|------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Status table of every upstream bead DDx filed/consumes | APPROVE | `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bead `ddx-d118f1bf` — description contains complete status table with filed date, upstream status, DDx bump commit, and DDx follow-up beads for agents 92f0f324, 767549c7, 941e7e42, 04639431, f237e07b, a365bcf2 |
| 2 | DDx-side consumer or smoke test for each landed fix | APPROVE | Same bead description — each status row has a consumer/coverage column (e.g. "ddx-4817edfd sub-beads...", "NO DDx CONSUMER NEEDED — the filter is entirely internal", "ddx-bbb2d177 smoke test landed in 1cd8e3a3 and was deleted in d5ebab2b") |
| 3 | Migration bead filed when agent-a365bcf2 lands, then closes | APPROVE | Status table correctly tracks agent-a365bcf2 as `in_progress`; remaining-work section (item 2) specifies the migration bead to file; `result.json` outcome `no_changes` is the correct signal — nothing to implement until upstream ships |

### Summary

This is a tracker/beacon bead; the "implementation" is the bead description itself. All three AC items are satisfied: the status table is complete and current, consumer coverage is documented per-row, and AC3 is correctly deferred pending upstream agent-a365bcf2. The `result.json` reports `outcome: no_changes` with an accurate rationale — no file edits are appropriate until the upstream preset rename lands.

### Findings

None.
